check.command: awk -F: 'NR==FNR{uid[$1]=$3; shell[$1]=$7; next} ($1 in uid) && uid[$1]>=1000 && shell[$1] !~ /(nologin|false)$/ && $4 < 1 {print $1 " " $4}' /etc/passwd /etc/shadow

candidate_check.description: OL 8 passwords for new users or password changes must have a 24 hours/one day minimum password lifetime restriction in "/etc/shadow".

check_content_excerpt:
Verify the minimum time period between password changes for each user account is one day or greater. 
 
$ sudo awk -F: '$4 < 1 {print $1 " " $4}' /etc/shadow 
 
If any results are returned that are not associated with a system account, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure non-compliant accounts to enforce a 24 hours/one day minimum password lifetime: 
 
$ sudo chage -m 1 [user]
title: OL 8 passwords for new users or password changes must have a 24 hours/one day minimum password lifetime restriction in "/etc/shadow".
